rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with expected types (int, real, bool) in binary comparisons and assignments.
Avoid using implementation-specific subcomponents or ports in component type annexes; use only visible features or declared helpers.
generic_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations in AGREE contracts.
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Ensure all AGREE expressions have well-defined types to prevent '<error>' type diagnostics.
Generate AGREE contracts using only visible AADL features and declared helpers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Binary expressions in AGREE must have operands of matching types (int, real, or bool).
Use nested 'if ... then ... else' for branching logic in AGREE.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined identifiers or use invalid type names in AGREE expressions.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not mix implementation-only subcomponent paths with component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
